asset_name: Retatrutide Discovery to Clinical Proof of Concept

study.citation: Coskun T, Urva S, Roell WC, et al. LY3437943, a novel triple glucagon, GIP, and GLP-1 receptor agonist for glycemic control and weight loss: From discovery to clinical proof of concept. Cell Metabolism. 2022;34(9):1234-1247.e9.

design.scope: Preclinical pharmacology plus first-in-human phase 1 single-ascending-dose evidence
design.primary_purpose: Mechanistic characterisation, safety/tolerability, pharmacokinetics and early proof of concept

key_findings: LY3437943 (retatrutide) demonstrated agonist activity at glucagon, GIP and GLP-1 receptors.
Preclinical experiments linked glucagon receptor activity to increased energy expenditure in the model studied.
The phase 1 clinical component reported a pharmacokinetic profile compatible with once-weekly dosing.
The paper reported body-weight reduction persisting through day 43 after a single dose in the phase 1 study.
limitations: The human clinical evidence was early phase and not designed to establish long-term efficacy.
Much of the mechanistic evidence was preclinical and should not be treated as direct proof of long-term human clinical outcomes.
The publication includes authors affiliated with the developer, Eli Lilly and Company.

source_verification.verification_note: Primary journal article page reviewed for title, authors, DOI, study scope, highlights and summary.
